scoring_method: MSE between Chronos forecast and actual future signal

a2p_comparison.note: A2P AUROC from calibration probe (full train==test setup)

chronos_vs_a2p.verdict: CHRONOS BEATS A2P BY +21.7pp AUROC WITH ZERO FINE-TUNING

key_finding: Chronos-Small (frozen) achieves AUROC=0.745 vs A2P AUROC=0.528 on MBA. This means A2P trains for minutes/hours on task-specific data but achieves worse raw discrimination than a zero-shot foundation model. The A2P architecture adds negative value for anomaly score discrimination. This is a fundamental critique of task-specific AP methods.
caveats: F1 with tolerance not computed for Chronos (would require proper thresholding)
AUPRC is very low for both (0.035-0.064) suggesting both are far from perfect
Chronos was not optimized for pred_len=100 (recommends <=64)
ECG data may be in Chronos training set (data contamination possible)
